etymology.description: 虫(蟲) (chóng, "insect") triplicated -> represents various insects
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 蟲 (chóng) combines three instances of 虫(蟲) (chóng, "insect") to convey the sense of a multitude or variety of insects, highlighting the character's association with small, creeping creatures.
etymology.simplification: 虫(蟲) was simplified by using its basic unit 虫 to represent the broader concept of insects collectively, reducing repetition.

examples.chinese: 花园里有很多虫(花園裡有很多蟲)。
examples.pinyin: Huāyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō chóng.
examples.english: There are many insects in the garden.

examples.chinese: 夏天的夜晚(夏天的夜晚)，虫鸣声很大(蟲鳴聲很大)。
examples.pinyin: Xiàtiān de yèwǎn, chóng míng shēng hěn dà.
examples.english: On summer nights, the sound of insects is very loud.

examples.chinese: 我在树林里(我在樹林裡)，发现了一只稀有的虫(發現了一隻稀有的蟲)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài shùlín lǐ, fāxiànle yī zhī xīyǒu de chóng.
examples.english: I discovered a rare insect in the forest.
